summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 15: re-detect against main (82 framework bugs fixed across rounds 0-14). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (88 sigs), adversarial verify. Round 14 found 2 (core validation ignored record-dependent layout visibility, widgets withFilters non-scalar serialization) so the consecutive-clean counter is 0/2. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 15 detection: 4 from clusters = 4 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 15 verify: 4 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: BelongsToField async picker is fully non-functional: searchRoute prop is never populated by any producer
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/core/src/Support/FieldSchemaSerializer.php:143 (serializeProps emits props raw; no searchRoute injection) + packages/fields/src/Types/BelongsToField.php:152-163 (getTypeSpecificProps omits searchRoute, defers to CORE-006 which shipped without it)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeProps (or InertiaDataBuilder where Resource/panel context is known), when the field is a BelongsToField inject `props['searchRoute'] = route('arqel.fields.search', ['resource' => $resourceSlug, 'field' => $field->getName()])` (and similarly preloadedOptions/createRoute). The route + FieldSearchController already exist; only the URL hand-off is missing. Add the previously-deferred BelongsToSearchTest feature test to lock it in.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced the full PHP->React contract dead-end statically. (1) packages-js/fields-js/src/relationship/BelongsToInput.tsx is async-only: the effect (line 43) short-circuits with setResults([]) when f.props.searchRoute is falsy, builds the fetch URL from it (line 52), and there is no preloaded-options/static fallback path. (2) searchRoute is produced NOWHERE: BelongsToField::getTypeSpecificProps() (packages/fields/src/Types/BelongsToField.php:152-163) emits relatedResource/relationship/searchable/searchColumns/preload only, with an explicit comment deferring searchRoute/preloadedOptions/createRoute to 'the controller (CORE-006)'. FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeProps() (packages/core/src/Support/FieldSchemaSerializer.php:143-161) copies getTypeSpecificProps() raw and injects nothing. InertiaDataBuilder (the form payload materialiser, lines 80/246/269/292) only calls serializer->serialize with no post-processing. grep across packages/core+packages/fields for 'searchRoute' yields only the BelongsToField.php comment — zero assignments. (3) The TS type packages-js/types/src/fields.ts:133-134 comments '/** Set client-side from the panel routes. */' but no JS sets it either: grep in fields-js/ui/core src finds only the 4 read-sites in BelongsToInput.tsx. So at runtime f.props.searchRoute is undefined, the picker renders but stays permanently empty and a belongsTo relation can never be selected via the searchable picker. The FieldSearchController (packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldSearchController.php) + route 'arqel.fields.search' (packages/fields/routes/arqel-fields.php:20-23) exist and are functional — they are simply never URL-fed to the client. NOT a still-open deferred placeholder: docs/tickets/current.md:426 records CORE-006 (ResourceController/InertiaDataBuilder) as SHIPPED, and the component is registered (packages-js/fields-js/src/register.ts:37) and exported — yet the searchRoute injection CORE-006 was documented (current.md:802,813) to deliver was never completed, and the deferred BelongsToSearchTest feature test never landed (no file BelongsToSearch* exists). app-misuse ruled out: the gap is in framework wiring (PHP serializer never emits the URL that the framework's own React component requires), independent of app code. Severity high not blocker: the showcase baseline sidesteps it by using a static Field::select('author_id')->options(...) (apps/showcase PostResource.php:83,108) rather than BelongsToField::make()->searchable(), so no current app crashes — but any consumer using the advertised searchable belongsTo picker gets a dead, unselectable field. Could not refute.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: PHP↔React contract dead-end. The React component packages-js/fields-js/src/relationship/BelongsToInput.tsx is hard-wired to f.props.searchRoute (line 43 guard `if (!f.props.searchRoute || !query) return;`, line 52 builds `${f.props.searchRoute}?q=...`, line 65 effect dep) with NO preloaded-options or static fallback path — it ONLY does async fetch. But searchRoute is produced NOWHERE: packages/fields/src/Types/BelongsToField.php::getTypeSpecificProps() (lines 152-164) emits relatedResource/relationship/searchable/searchColumns/preload and a comment explicitly says searchRoute/preloadedOptions/createRoute are 'filled in by the controller (CORE-006)'; packages/core/src/Support/FieldSchemaSerializer.php::serializeProps() (lines 143-161) only copies getTypeSpecificProps() raw and injects nothing; grep across packages/core+packages/fields for 'searchRoute' yields only the BelongsToField.php comment (no assignment). The TS type packages-js/types/src/fields.ts:133-134 contradicts the PHP, commenting '/** Set client-side from the panel routes. */' yet no JS sets it either (grep in fields-js/ui/src finds only the 4 read-sites in BelongsToInput.tsx). Net: f.props.searchRoute is undefined at runtime, the effect short-circuits, the dropdown stays permanently empty, and a belongsTo relation can never be selected in any create/edit form. The FieldSearchController + route ('arqel.fields.search', packages/fields/routes/arqel-fields.php) exist and work — they're just never URL-fed to the client. Repro: render any Resource form with BelongsToField::make('author_id', AuthorResource::class)->searchable(); the picker renders but typing returns zero results because searchRoute is undefined. CORE-006 (the controller that was supposed to inject this) shipped (docs/tickets/current.md:426) without completing this wiring.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: SelectField::optionsRelationship() renders a permanently empty <select>: relation options are never resolved into props.options
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/fields/src/Types/SelectField.php:170-183 (resolveOptions returns [] for relationship mode); compounded by packages/core/src/Support/FieldSchemaSerializer.php:143-161 (serializeProps copies raw, never resolves optionsRelation) and packages-js/fields-js/src/select/SelectInput.tsx:25 (reads only props.options)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Resolve the relation server-side: have the ResourceController (or FieldSchemaSerializer with owner-Resource/model context) detect fields where getOptionsRelation() !== null, run `$ownerModel->{$relation}()->getRelated()` query (applying optionsRelationQuery, plucking display => key) and inject the result into props.options before serialisation — then update the SelectFieldTest line 54 assertion that currently codifies resolveOptions()===[] for the relationship case. Alternatively, ship a relation-aware endpoint the React SelectInput fetches from when props.optionsRelation is set.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced the full dead-UI chain on current main. (1) SelectField::resolveOptions() (SelectField.php:170-183) handles only staticOptions and optionsCallback; the relationship branch falls through to `return []`. (2) getTypeSpecificProps() (264-275) emits `options => []` and a separate unused `optionsRelation` string. (3) FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeProps() (FieldSchemaSerializer.php:143-161) copies props raw and resolves nothing; grep confirms NO consumer of getOptionsRelation()/optionsRelation exists in packages/core (ResourceController/InertiaDataBuilder included). (4) ResourceController uses effectiveFields()+serializer with no relation enrichment. (5) React SelectInput.tsx:25 renders strictly from f.props.options and never consults props.optionsRelation. Net: Field::select('category_id')->optionsRelationship('category','name') renders a permanently empty <select>. The static and closure option modes work, so only the relationship mode (advertised in the class docblock line 16, SKILL.md, and fields.md docs in 3 languages as a first-class option mode) is broken. The 'deferred to CORE-006' docblock excuse is STALE: current.md:426 confirms CORE-006 shipped (ResourceController, FieldSchemaSerializer, InertiaDataBuilder delivered) without ever wiring the resolution. This is the documented-but-unwired + tests-mask-integration-gaps systemic family — not a properly-tracked deferred feature like EXPORT-007/008. SelectFieldTest.php:54 even asserts resolveOptions()===[] for the relationship case, codifying the broken behavior. Severity medium (not high): degrades gracefully to an empty control rather than crashing, and the baseline showcase app does not currently use optionsRelationship so there is no live in-app failure — but any framework consumer using the public, documented API gets a non-functional select.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Documented-but-unwired option resolution. SelectField advertises an 'Eloquent relationship' option mode (packages/fields/src/Types/SelectField.php docblock line 16: `optionsRelationship('category', 'name')`). But SelectField::resolveOptions() (lines 170-183) handles ONLY staticOptions and optionsCallback and returns [] for the relationship case; getTypeSpecificProps() (lines 264-275) therefore emits `options => []` plus a separate `optionsRelation => 'category'`. FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeProps() copies these raw and resolves nothing. The React component packages-js/fields-js/src/select/SelectInput.tsx (line 25 `const options = normaliseOptions(f.props.options);`) renders strictly from props.options and never consults props.optionsRelation. Result: a Resource field declared as Field::select('category_id')->optionsRelationship('category','name') renders a <select> with ZERO options — the relationship mode is dead UI. Same root cause as the BelongsToField gap: 'resolução adiada para CORE-006 controller' (docs/tickets/current.md:837) but CORE-006 shipped without wiring it. Distinct entry point (SelectField/SelectInput, not BelongsTo). Note: the static/closure option modes work, so only the relationship mode is broken.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: AiGenerate/AiClassify/AiTranslate controllers don't catch AiException -> provider failure or cost-limit returns unhandled HTTP 500 (asymmetric with Extract/AnalyzeImage which return 422)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Wrap the field call in each of the three controllers in `try { ... } catch (\Arqel\Ai\Exceptions\AiException $e) { return new JsonResponse(['message' => $e->getMessage()], 422); }`, mirroring AiExtractController:85-89 and AiAnalyzeImageController:96-100; add provider-failure feature tests for all three.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Verified at source: AiExtractController (85-89) and AiAnalyzeImageController (96-100) catch AiException and return 422, but AiGenerateController (line 72 `$aiField->generate($formData)`), AiClassifyController (line 80 `$selectField->classify($formData)`), and AiTranslateController (96-100 `$translateField->translate(...)` loop) call the field methods with no try/catch. The exception chain is real and reachable: generate/classify/translate -> AiManager::complete() which at AiManager.php:59 calls costTracker->assertWithinLimit() (throws DailyLimitExceeded/UserLimitExceeded, both `extends AiException` per Exceptions/*.php:12) and at line 62 calls provider->complete() (can throw AiException). AiException is a bare RuntimeException with no render()/getStatusCode(), and no withExceptions/renderable handler maps it (grep found none), so Laravel renders it as HTTP 500. Thus a real provider upstream error or a configured cost cap surfaces as an unhandled 500 on 3 of 5 sibling AI endpoints versus the clean 422 the other two return. Tests corroborate the gap: AiExtractControllerTest has a deliberate 'returns 422 when AI response cannot be parsed as JSON' test exercising its catch, while AiGenerate/Classify/TranslateControllerTest contain zero AiException/provider-failure assertions (grep returned nothing). This is framework code (the controllers ship in arqel-dev/ai), so it is not app-misuse; the contract is internally inconsistent. Not a blocker because it is latent in the showcase baseline (no AI field wired into a Resource; StubProvider reports $0 cost so the cap never trips), but any consumer wiring an AI field with a real provider or a cost cap is exposed. Distinct from the round-5 fields()-vs-effectiveFields() lookup fix (that was a 404/422 field-lookup bug; this is exception handling on the field-invocation path).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Live repro in apps/showcase via tinker: bound an AiManager whose provider->complete()/chat() throws Arqel\Ai\Exceptions\AiException('provider upstream 503'), registered an anonymous Resource exposing AiTextField+AiExtractField+AiSelectField+AiTranslateField, and invoked each single-action controller. Results: GENERATE THREW (=> HTTP 500) Arqel\Ai\Exceptions\AiException; CLASSIFY THREW (=> HTTP 500); TRANSLATE THREW (=> HTTP 500); EXTRACT returned status 422 body {"message":"provider upstream 503"}; ANALYZE_IMAGE handled. Root cause: AiExtractController (packages/ai/src/Http/Controllers/AiExtractController.php:87-88) and AiAnalyzeImageController (AiAnalyzeImageController.php:~98) wrap the field call in `try { ... } catch (AiException $e) { return 422 }`, but AiGenerateController (AiGenerateController.php: `$text = $aiField->generate($formData);` with no try/catch), AiClassifyController (`$key = $selectField->classify($formData);`), and AiTranslateController (`$translateField->translate(...)` loop) do NOT. The field methods call AiManager::complete()/chat(), which (a) runs CostTracker::assertWithinLimit() that throws DailyLimitExceeded / UserLimitExceeded (both extend AiException, packages/ai/src/Exceptions/*.php) when arqel-ai.cost_tracking limits are hit, and (b) calls provider->complete() which can throw AiException on upstream/API failure. So a real provider error or a configured cost cap surfaces to the Inertia/HTTP layer as an unhandled 500 on 3 of the 5 sibling AI endpoints, instead of the clean 422 the other two return. Distinct from the round-5 fields()-vs-effectiveFields() AI lookup fix (that was a 404/422 lookup bug, not exception handling). Latent in showcase baseline only because no AI field is wired into a Resource there and the StubProvider reports $0 cost so the cap never trips; any consumer wiring an AI field with a real provider (claude/openai/ollama) or any cost cap is exposed.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Exporters ignore Column getStateUsing/formatStateUsing/ComputedColumn — exported cells diverge from displayed table (computed cols export blank, formatted cols export raw)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/export/src/Exporters/CsvExporter.php:114 (and XlsxExporter.php:~125, PdfExporter.php:~144) — formatCell() resolves via data_get($record, $name), bypassing Column::getState()/formatState(); upstream packages/core/src/Support/InertiaDataBuilder.php:555 (serializeRecord builds rows from $record->toArray(), also bypassing getState/formatState) and packages/table/src/Column.php:360 (toArray() strips both closures)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Resolve cell state through the Column pipeline instead of raw data_get: have the controller/ExportAction pass the actual Column objects (not just toArray() descriptors) so the exporter can call $column->formatState($column->getState($record), $record); equivalently, fix it at the source by having InertiaDataBuilder::serializeRecord inject computed/formatted cells (getState→formatState per column) into the row payload so BOTH the React table and any data_get-based exporter see the resolved value. Add export + serializeRecord tests covering ComputedColumn/getStateUsing/formatStateUsing.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: CONFIRMED the exporters never honor getStateUsing/formatStateUsing: CsvExporter::formatCell (line 114) and the Xlsx/Pdf twins resolve via data_get($record,$name); Column::toArray() (line 360-380) strips both closures, and ResourceController::bulkAction (line 188-206) hands raw Eloquent records + closure-stripped column arrays to ExportAction. ComputedColumn cells export blank (no such attribute) and formatStateUsing cells export raw. So getState()/formatState() (Column.php:302-327) are genuinely dead in the export path. This is a real framework gap. HOWEVER the candidate's central framing — that exported cells DIVERGE from the displayed table — is REFUTED. The display path is equally broken: InertiaDataBuilder::serializeRecord (line 555) builds the row payload from $record->toArray() and never calls getState()/formatState(), and the React DataTable (DataTable.tsx:69) reads accessorFn: row=>row[col.name]. A ComputedColumn('full_title') therefore renders BLANK in the live table too, and a formatStateUsing column renders RAW in the table too. getState()/formatState() are invoked nowhere outside their own Column unit tests (grep across core+table src is empty). So nothing diverges — the export merely mirrors a framework-wide gap where these closures are never wired into serialization. The candidate built a synthetic repro; no showcase resource uses getStateUsing/formatStateUsing/ComputedColumn. Net: real bug (these column features are non-functional end-to-end), but mis-scoped/mis-attributed as an export-local divergence. Distinct from the Round-9 RelationshipColumn display_path fix (that handles type==='relationship' only). Severity downgraded high→medium: uniform non-functionality of an opt-in, unused-in-showcase feature, not silent export-only data corruption behind a correct-looking UI.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Live repro in apps/showcase: built ComputedColumn::make('full_title')->getStateUsing(fn($r)=>'COMPUTED:'.$r->title) and TextColumn::make('title')->formatStateUsing(fn($s,$r)=>strtoupper($s)), serialized via column->toArray() exactly like ResourceController::serializeColumns does, then ran (new CsvExporter)->export([$post], $columns, $dest). CSV output: header 'Full Title,Title' then row ',audit-version-probe-1780882405' — the computed column cell is BLANK (closure never invoked) and the title cell is the RAW lowercase value, not the formatStateUsing uppercase transform. Root cause: all three exporters (packages/export/src/Exporters/{Csv,Xlsx,Pdf}Exporter.php formatCell()) resolve cell values with data_get($record, $name) directly, completely bypassing the canonical table render pipeline Column::getState() (honors getStateUsing, used by ComputedColumn for which $record->getAttribute('full_title') is null) and Column::formatState() (honors formatStateUsing) in packages/table/src/Column.php:302-327. Additionally Column::toArray() strips both closures (verified: serialized COLUMNS JSON contains no getStateUsing/formatStateUsing keys), and ResourceController::bulkAction (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:188-206) passes raw Eloquent records plus those closure-stripped column arrays to ExportAction::execute, so the exporter has no access to the state/format logic the table used to render the same data. No export test covers computed/state columns (grep of packages/export/tests for computed|getStateUsing|formatStateUsing|ComputedColumn returns nothing), so the gap is unmasked. Distinct entry point from the Round-9 RelationshipColumn display_path fix: that handled type==='relationship', but computed/getStateUsing/formatStateUsing are entirely separate, dead in the export path.
